title: HDFC Millennia Credit Card Review (2026): Is It Still the Best Cashback Credit Card in India?
content:
HDFC Millennia Credit Card Review (2026): Is It Still the Best Cashback Credit Card in India?

If you're searching for a credit card that rewards your everyday online shopping, the HDFC Millennia Credit Card is one of the strongest options in India. Designed primarily for millennials and digital-first users, this card offers cashback on popular online brands, milestone benefits, and lifestyle perks.

But is it really worth paying the annual fee?

After using and analyzing the card's benefits, fees, reward structure, and limitations, here's a complete review.

Quick Rating

CategoryRatingCashback⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)Online Shopping⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)Travel Benefits⭐⭐⭐⭐☆ (4/5)Lounge Access⭐⭐⭐☆☆ (3/5)Annual Fee⭐⭐⭐⭐☆ (4/5)Overall⭐⭐⭐⭐☆ (4.6/5)HDFC Millennia Credit Card Overview

  • Card Type: Cashback Credit Card
  • Best For: Online Shopping
  • Joining Fee: ₹1,000 + GST
  • Annual Fee: ₹1,000 + GST
  • Renewal Fee Waiver: Spend ₹1 lakh in a year
  • Interest-Free Period: Up to 50 days

For most users who spend regularly on shopping, food delivery, and entertainment, the annual fee can easily be recovered through cashback.

Cashback Benefits

The biggest reason people choose this card is its cashback program.

5% Cashback

You earn 5% cashback on select partner merchants such as:

  • Amazon
  • Flipkart
  • Myntra
  • Swiggy
  • Zomato
  • Uber
  • BookMyShow
  • Tata CLiQ
  • Sony LIV
  • Cult.fit

The cashback on these merchants is capped per statement cycle.

1% Cashback

You also earn:

  • 1% cashback on most other eligible purchases
  • Includes many everyday spends

This makes the card useful even when you're not shopping at partner merchants.

Annual Fee

Joining Fee:

₹1,000 + GST

Renewal Fee:

₹1,000 + GST

Good news:

If you spend ₹1,00,000 in one year, the renewal fee is waived.

Welcome Benefits

After paying the joining fee, eligible cardholders receive welcome benefits equivalent to the fee amount in the form specified by HDFC Bank's current offer. Offers may change over time, so check the latest terms before applying.

Milestone Benefits

Spend:

₹1 lakh in a calendar quarter

and receive

₹1,000 worth of gift vouchers from eligible brands.

Dining Benefits

The card also provides discounts on eligible restaurant payments through Swiggy Dineout offers, subject to current promotional terms.

Fuel Surcharge Waiver

You receive:

  • 1% fuel surcharge waiver
  • Subject to transaction limits and maximum monthly waiver

Useful if you regularly fuel your vehicle.

Airport Lounge Access

The Millennia card offers lounge access benefits, but the eligibility and access rules depend on HDFC Bank's prevailing policy and spending requirements. Always verify the latest terms before relying on this feature.

Pros

✅ Excellent cashback on online shopping

✅ Simple cashback structure

✅ Fee waiver at ₹1 lakh annual spend

✅ Great for Amazon and Flipkart users

✅ Milestone gift vouchers

✅ Dining discounts

✅ Fuel surcharge waiver

Cons

❌ Annual fee if spending is low

❌ Cashback limits on partner brands

❌ Not ideal for international spending due to forex markup

❌ Travel benefits are limited compared to premium cards

Who Should Get This Card?

This card is perfect if you:

  • Shop online regularly
  • Use Amazon and Flipkart frequently
  • Order from Swiggy and Zomato
  • Watch movies via BookMyShow
  • Want straightforward cashback instead of complicated reward points
  • Spend at least ₹1 lakh annually

Who Should Avoid It?

Consider another card if:

  • You rarely shop online
  • You travel internationally often
  • You want premium airport lounge access
  • You mainly spend on fuel or utilities

Is It Better Than SBI Cashback Credit Card?

The HDFC Millennia Credit Card is a strong choice if much of your spending is on its partner merchants and you also value HDFC-specific offers.

The SBI Cashback Credit Card generally offers a broader cashback structure across many online merchants, making it attractive for users who shop across a wider variety of websites.

Your spending pattern should determine which card gives better value.

Final Verdict

The HDFC Millennia Credit Card remains one of India's best entry-level cashback credit cards for digital shoppers. If your monthly expenses include Amazon, Flipkart, Swiggy, Zomato, Myntra, Uber, and similar platforms, it's easy to recover the annual fee and earn meaningful cashback.

However, if your goal is premium travel perks or international usage, a travel-focused credit card may be a better fit.

Overall Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐☆ (4.6/5)

For students, young professionals, and anyone who shops online frequently, the HDFC Millennia Credit Card is still one of the easiest cashback cards to recommend.
excerpt: the HDFC Millennia Credit Card is one of the strongest options in India. Designed primarily for millennials and digital-first users, this card offers cashback on popular online brands, milestone benefits, and lifestyle perks.  But is it really worth paying the annual fee?
